Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Datahäxan 103 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 166 upvotes, 18 comments
  3. Printf-Tac-Toe 75 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. WebPKI and You 76 upvotes, 9 comments
  5. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 129 upvotes, 28 comments
  6. 1B identity records exposed in ID verification data leak 147 upvotes, 33 comments
  7. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3886 upvotes, 1451 comments
  8. High fidelity font synthesis for CJK languages 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 198 upvotes, 65 comments
  10. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 157 upvotes, 57 comments
  11. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 362 upvotes, 162 comments
  12. ATMs didn't kill bank Teller jobs, but the iPhone did 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 39 upvotes, 15 comments
  14. ArcaOS 5.1.2 (based on OS/2 Warp 4.52) now available 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  15. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B (2025) 85 upvotes, 45 comments
  16. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 89 upvotes, 48 comments
  17. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 44 upvotes, 20 comments
  18. Returning to Rails in 2026 250 upvotes, 161 comments
  19. Suburban school district uses license plate readers to verify student residency 43 upvotes, 21 comments
  20. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 61 upvotes, 36 comments
  21. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 13 upvotes, 4 comments
  22. Show HN: We analyzed 1,573 Claude Code sessions to see how AI agents work 53 upvotes, 31 comments
  23. Big Data on the Cheapest MacBook 191 upvotes, 159 comments
  24. Show HN: Axe A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 26 upvotes, 16 comments
  25. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 381 upvotes, 385 comments
  26. USDA is closing buildings, relocating staff, and downsizing-a lot 25 upvotes, 17 comments
  27. The purpose of Continuous Integration is to fail 17 upvotes, 20 comments
  28. Kotlin creator's new language: a formal way to talk to LLMs instead of English 7 upvotes, 6 comments
  29. Show HN: Calyx – Ghostty-Based macOS Terminal with Liquid Glass UI 14 upvotes, 20 comments
  30. Hive (YC S14) is hiring scrappy product managers and product/data engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 12 March 2026. Source here.
